studies.title: Cross-Domain Temporal Clustering Atlas — Inter-Event Time Analysis Across Environmental Metrics
studies.description: Applying the coefficient of variation (CV) inter-event time method proven on earthquakes (CV=1.545) to CMEs, radiation spikes, geomagnetic storms, AQI exceedances, and flood events. Building a clustering atlas across domains.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: ENSO Phase vs US Drought Severity
studies.description: Investigating whether El Niño/La Niña phases predict drought severity in US states. Using 76 years of ONI data and 16 years of USDM drought records.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: Temporal Clustering of Global M4+ Earthquakes
studies.description: Analyzing whether significant earthquakes cluster in time beyond what random (Poisson) occurrence would predict. Testing the hypothesis of earthquake triggering — does one large quake increase the probability of subsequent events?
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: Solar Activity and Earthquake Frequency — Is There a Signal?
studies.description: Testing the controversial hypothesis that solar activity (CMEs, solar flux, geomagnetic storms) correlates with earthquake frequency or magnitude. Using NOAA SWPC, NASA DONKI, and USGS earthquake data.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

references.title: Deep Search for Joint Sources of Gravitational Waves and High-Energy Neutrinos with IceCube During the Third Observing Run of LIGO and Virgo
references.abstract: The discovery of joint sources of high-energy neutrinos and gravitational waves has been a primary target for the LIGO, Virgo, KAGRA, and IceCube observatories. The joint detection of high-energy neutrinos and gravitational waves would provide insight into cosmic processes, from the dynamics of comp

references.title: The solar-stellar connection: Magnetic activity of seismic solar analogs
references.abstract: Finding solar-analog stars with fundamental properties as close as possible to the Sun and studying the characteristics of their surface magnetic activity is a very promising way to understand the solar variability and its associated dynamo process. However, the identification of solar-analog stars

references.title: Observation of the rare $B^0_s\toμ^+μ^-$ decay from the combined analysis of CMS and LHCb data
references.abstract: A joint measurement is presented of the branching fractions $B^0_s\toμ^+μ^-$ and $B^0\toμ^+μ^-$ in proton-proton collisions at the LHC by the CMS and LHCb experiments. The data samples were collected in 2011 at a centre-of-mass energy of 7 TeV, and in 2012 at 8 TeV. The combined analysis produces th

references.title: Solar dynamo and geomagnetic activity
references.abstract: The correlation between geomagnetic activity and the sunspot number in the 11-year solar cycle exhibits long-term variations due to the varying time lag between the sunspot-related and non-sunspot related geomagnetic activity, and the varying relative amplitude of the respective geomagnetic activity

studies.title: ENSO Teleconnection to Global Seismicity — Replication Study
studies.description: Replicating and extending published claims that El Niño/La Niña phases modulate global earthquake rates. Testing crustal loading hypothesis via sea level redistribution. Using 76 years of ONI data and 5 years of USGS M4+ earthquake records.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

references.title: Intra-annual Principal Modes and Evolution Mechanism of the El Nino Southern Oscillation
references.abstract: The El Nino-Southern Oscillation (ENSO) is one of the most important phenomena in climate. By studying the fluctuations of surface air temperature within one year between 1979-01-01 and 2016-12-31 of the region (30S-30N, 0E-360E) with eigen-decomposition, we find that the temperature fluctuations ar

references.title: Complexity based approach for El Nino magnitude forecasting before the "spring predictability barrier"
references.abstract: The El Nino Southern Oscillation (ENSO) is one of the most prominent interannual climate phenomena. An early and reliable ENSO forecasting remains a crucial goal, due to its serious implications for economy, society, and ecosystem. Despite the development of various dynamical and statistical predict

references.title: Application of Differential Transform Method for El Nino Southern Oscillation (ENSO) Model with compared Adomian Decomposition and Variational Iteration Methods
references.abstract: We consider two nonlinear El Nino Southern Oscillation (ENSO) model to obtain approximate solutions with differential transform method for the first time. Efficiency, accuracy and error rates of solutions are compared with analytic solution, variational iteration and adomian decomposition solutions

references.title: El Nino signature in Alaskan river breakups
references.abstract: A signature of El Nino-Southern Oscillation is found in the historical dataset of the Alaskan Tanana river breakups where the average ice breaking day is found to anticipate of about 3.4 days when conditioned over El Nino years. This results represents a statistically significant example of ENSO tel

studies.title: Solar Flux–Kp Index Time Lag — Paper Replication Study
studies.description: Replicating Georgieva & Kirov (2010) claim that the correlation between solar activity and geomagnetic activity varies with a changing time lag. Testing with NOAA SWPC solar flux and Kp index data.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: Compound Environmental Anomalies — Multi-Metric Unusual Days
studies.description: Detecting days where multiple environmental metrics are simultaneously anomalous using Z-score analysis and Isolation Forest. When earthquakes, space weather, and air quality all spike together — is it coincidence or connection?
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: Temporal Clustering of Global M4+ Earthquakes — Spatial clustering
studies.description:
Derivative study expanding on: Spatial clustering. nearest-neighbor distance analysis

Parent study: Temporal Clustering of Global M4+ Earthquakes (earthquake-temporal-clustering)
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: Global Environmental Radiation Baseline from Citizen Science — Signal vs Sampling Bias
studies.description: Separating physical radiation patterns from observational campaign clustering in 679K Safecast measurements across 45 countries. Can citizen science data establish a reliable global radiation baseline?
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: March 2026 Fireball Cluster — Statistical Significance and Space Weather Context
studies.description: Testing whether the March 2026 fireball cluster (11 events in 20 days, 3 meteorite falls) is statistically significant against the CNEOS historical baseline. Cross-correlating with TerraPulse space weather data to test solar activity connection.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: Urban Temperature Trends — Are Cities Warming in Real-Time Data?
studies.description: Testing whether 3 years of NASA POWER daily temperature data (10 cities) shows detectable warming trends. Using linear regression, seasonal decomposition, and anomaly scoring.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: California Streamflow Anomaly Detection
studies.description: Detect anomalous streamflow patterns across 472 USGS California gauging stations
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Cross-Domain Granger Causality Network
studies.description: Test which environmental signals Granger-cause others — the big connectivity map
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Air Quality–Weather Coupling
studies.description: Test whether temperature patterns predict PM2.5 spikes via inversion-like conditions
studies.author: claude

studies.title: US Tornado Climatology 1950-2023
studies.description: 73-year analysis of tornado patterns, Tornado Alley migration, EF trends, and solar-tornado coupling
studies.author: claude

studies.title: LIGO–Environment Coupling
studies.description: Do earthquakes, ocean microseisms, and geomagnetic storms correlate with gravitational wave detector sensitivity?
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Volcano-Earthquake Coupling
studies.description: Do earthquakes cluster near active volcanoes? Can seismic swarms predict eruptions?
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Solar Forcing of Terrestrial Geophysics
studies.description: Do solar flares, CMEs, and the solar cycle directly drive earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, and severe weather? The definitive test.
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Earthquake–Tidal Forcing
studies.description: Do earthquakes cluster at Full Moon, New Moon, or lunar perigee? Testing the 1-2% tidal triggering hypothesis with 172K earthquakes and 5K hours of JPL ephemeris.
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Universal Scaling Laws
studies.description: Cross-domain distribution analysis: power law vs exponential vs lognormal vs Weibull across 10 environmental metrics
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Tornado Season Shift Analysis
studies.description: Is tornado season extending later into the year? 73 years of SPC data.
studies.author: claude

studies.title: NEO Gravitational Null Test
studies.description: Superposed epoch analysis: do near-Earth object close approaches produce detectable changes in earthquake rate, Kp, or tides?
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Geomagnetic Storm Cascades
studies.description: When Kp spikes, what else moves? Trace the cascade through AQI, earthquakes, tides, radiation, power systems, and atmospheric chemistry.
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Tidal Forcing on Volcanic Eruptions
studies.description: Schuster test: do volcanoes erupt at New/Full Moon?
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: CO2 Seasonal Cycle and Volcanic Eruptions
studies.description: Can we detect Pinatubo (1991), El Chichon (1982) in the Keeling Curve? Cross-reference CO2 with 5K eruption records.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: CO2 and Temperature Trend
studies.description: Does the Keeling Curve predict temperature anomaly? 52yr CO2 vs temperature data.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: LIGO/Virgo as accidental geophysical sensors — GW detector sensitivity vs space weather
studies.description: Do geomagnetic storms degrade gravitational wave detector sensitivity? Can we turn LIGO/Virgo duty cycle data into an accidental geophysical measurement?
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: X1.5 Solar Flare Watch — March 29, 2026
studies.description: X1.5 flare (largest in 12.6M GOES readings) + 1,845 km/s CME. 6-entry lab notebook tracking the event over 60h. CME did not impact Earth. Successful infrastructure test of 7-stream monitoring pipeline.
studies.author: Lab Notebook

studies.title: NWS Severe Weather Baseline — Alert Patterns and Geographic Distribution
studies.description: Baseline analysis of 550K NWS alerts (45 types, 8 days) + 69K historical tornado reports (1950-2023). 80% marine, 63x diurnal swing, May tornado peak, Kp cross-domain null. Foundation for dashboard and cross-domain work.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: DONKI Event Catalog — Cascade Triggers in the Sun-to-Ground Chain
studies.description: SEA of 1,809 DONKI events: storms peak Kp at T+6h (+47%), HSS +6%, radiation belt lags by 38h. CME speed negatively correlates with Kp (r=-0.18) due to catalog selection bias.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: WSPR Ionospheric Propagation Baseline — 21 Years of Global HF Health
studies.description: 11.5B WSPR spots (2004-2026), 108K daily band aggregates. 40m/20m improve in solar max (r=+0.45), 10m degrades (r=-0.29). Network grew from 4 TX to 771. The ionosphere breathes with the Sun.
studies.author: Lab

studies.title: WSPR Anomaly Detection — Transient Hunting in 21 Years of Ionospheric Residuals
studies.description: Cycle removal + anomaly detection on 108K WSPR rows finds 202 transient events. 17 attributed to known storms, 66 unexplained (statistically identical to attributed, p=0.71). Method validated by blind detection of May 2024 G5 superstorm (z=6.7σ).
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: WSPR Transient Deep Dive — Cross-Reference Unexplained Events
studies.description: 3 of 32 Cat C events reclassified (G5 storm, solar eclipse, Grimsvotn eruption). 29 remain unexplained: 79% positive enhancement (p=0.002), 55% at solar minimum, upper HF dominant. Sporadic F-region density enhancements.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: NUFORC Cross-Correlation — Filter UFO Sightings Against Known Phenomena
studies.description: 80K NUFORC sightings filtered: 6.2% self-classified fireballs (exclusive), 16.3% storm/aurora (exclusive), 0 CNEOS matches. 75.9% unexplained with strong summer seasonal bias (chi2(11)=3466, days-per-month null). Storm match rate (19.5% of covered) below random baseline (31.2%).
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: WSPR Storm Response — Corridor SNR During Geomagnetic Storms
studies.description: POLAR paths degrade 0.29 dB during geomagnetic storms (t=4.9, p=2e-6, d=-0.35, N=200 per-storm deltas). EQUAT null (Wilcoxon p=0.088, Bonferroni p=0.35) validates corridor-differential method. Differential POLAR-EQUAT = -0.23 dB. Effect monotonically increases with storm severity (d=-0.62 at Dst<-100). NA_EU nominally significant (p=0.024) but does not survive Bonferroni correction.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Multi-network seismic comparison — USGS vs EMSC vs GFZ vs ISC catalog completeness
studies.description: We now ingest from 4 seismic networks. Compare: detection thresholds by region, magnitude agreement for shared events, temporal coverage gaps.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Magnetic pole drift vs WSPR 21-year propagation trend
studies.description: The magnetic north pole has accelerated toward Siberia (~55 km/yr). Our WSPR data shows -0.1 dB/year across all HF bands over 21 years.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: ENSO-Severe Weather Coupling: NWS Alerts vs El Nino/La Nina Phases
studies.description: Does ENSO modulate NWS alert rates? Monthly alert counts stratified by ONI phase. La Nina -> more tornadoes? El Nino -> more floods? Fire weather vs drought index.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Starlink Cannot Explain Pre-2014 UFO Sightings: Satellite Phenotype Characterization
studies.description: Starlink attribution to NUFORC sightings is impossible: NUFORC ends 2014, Starlink launched 2019. Pre-2014 satellite phenotype analysis: 826 sightings (1.03%) with explicit satellite mentions, 3,862 (4.81%) matching broad satellite phenotype. Formation shape increased 1.6x post-Iridium (chi2(1)=23.0, p=1.6e-6, N=76,097). Even broadest filter reduces unexplained from 75.9% to 71.1%. Starlink (10,119 sats) would dominate post-2019 reports but cannot be tested against this dataset.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Radon precursor hypothesis — Safecast radiation vs earthquake timing
studies.description: Does background radiation (Safecast citizen-science network) show detectable anomalies in the days before significant earthquakes?
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Lunar tidal signal in inland rivers -- gravitational forcing on USGS streamflow
studies.description: Can we detect a lunar tidal signal in inland river streamflow? The gravitational tidal force acts on all mass, not just oceans -- but the effect on rivers should be extremely small.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Solar Cycle Prediction: Can 208 Years of Sunspot Data Beat NOAA Forecasts?
studies.description: Can simple time-series methods (ARIMA, spectral decomposition, or ML) on our 208-year sunspot record produce a competitive forecast for Solar Cycle 25? Benchmark against NOAA predicted range.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: NEO close approaches — flyby distance/velocity patterns and fireball correlation
studies.description: Do near-Earth object close approach patterns correlate with fireball events? Are there temporal clusters in flyby distance or velocity?
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: NWS Alert Cascades: Cross-Domain Temporal Prediction Networks
studies.description: Do flood warnings predict landslide advisories? Do heat advisories predict air quality alerts? Build a temporal cascade network from the NWS alert stream.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Earthquake-Weather Coupling: Barometric Pressure Trigger Hypothesis
studies.description: Test whether rapid drops in atmospheric pressure trigger small earthquakes. The folk-science hypothesis says storm fronts reduce confining stress on shallow faults and should elevate seismicity by 0.1-1%. Controlled studies generally find no effect.
studies.author: claude

studies.title: Granger V4 — Full Environmental Network with CO2, CH4, Tidal Proxy, Pressure
studies.description: V4 extends the TerraPulse Granger-causality network analysis (V1-V3) by adding atmospheric composition (CO2, CH4), a lunar tidal-potential proxy, and barometric pressure to the 8-metric space-weather+surface network from V3. Emphasis is on honest per-pair overlap accounting, Bonferroni correction based on tests actually run, joint F-tests over all lags, stationarity checks, a shuffle null, and AR self-predictability baselines.
studies.author: claude

studies.title: WSPR Ionospheric Geography — Global Propagation Atlas from 11.5B Spots
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: WSPR Solar Cycle Modulation — How the 11-Year Cycle Reshapes HF Propagation
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Two Competing Responses Hidden in the 10 m WSPR Anticorrelation: Station-Pair Validation Reveals a Short-Path versus Long-Path Sign Flip
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Can WSPR Detect Aircraft? A Pre-Registered Test Against ADS-B Ground Truth
studies.description: Before evaluating any WSPR-aircraft tracking hypothesis (e.g., MH370), we must answer the prior question: can WSPR detect commercial aircraft at all? This pre-registered analysis tests whether WSPR SNR variations correlate with known flight crossings of station-pair great circle paths.
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: April 2026 Offshore Washington Swarm: Ridge, Not Subduction Zone
studies.description: Fact-check of claims that an earthquake swarm off Washington coast is occurring in the Cascadia Subduction Zone. USGS data shows the swarm at ~129°W on the Juan de Fuca Ridge, ~400 km west of the CSZ trench.
studies.author: TerraPulse

studies.title: Kermadec Doublet — M6.0 + M5.9 same epicenter, 48h apart
studies.description: On 2026-04-16 a M6.0 earthquake struck south of the Kermadec Islands (-32.21, -178.05, depth 10 km). 48 hours later (2026-04-18), a M5.9 struck the same
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Lunar Tidal Forcing Signature in USGS Streamflow
studies.description: Does the lunar gravitational tide produce a detectable periodic signal in inland river streamflow at M2 period (12.42h)?
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: Solar Wind to Kp Storm Onset Lag Time Distribution
studies.description: Research question: What is the empirical distribution of lag times between a southward IMF Bz excursion (DSCOVR L1 measurement) and the resulting Kp index increase indicating geomagnetic storm onset?
studies.author: PMA

studies.title: WSPR tornado season — CONUS 10-day SNR/density map with severe weather overlay
studies.description: Does WSPR HF propagation show a detectable SNR/density depression in grid cells under active severe weather? 10-day CONUS dataset with SPC + NWS overlay. GH issues #133-138.
studies.author: bisenbek

studies.title: WSPR tornado V2: historical outbreak case studies (Joplin, Super Outbreak, Moore, Rolling Fork, ...)
studies.description: V2 verification of the paper #29 40m precursor finding using 7 historical outbreaks + 21 matched controls
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: WSPR tornado V3: calm-season + density-matched controls (12 outbreaks, 24 controls)
studies.description: V3 with fixed control design — calm-season controls, SPC-pre-screened, density-matched
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab

studies.title: WSPR tornado V4: mechanism test — WSPR × GOES-16 GLM lightning cross-correlation
studies.description: V4 discriminates D-layer absorption (WSPR leads lightning) from QRN (simultaneous) using GLM historical lightning data on the V3 outbreaks. Data source: GOES-16 GLM L2 LCFA from noaa-goes16 public S3 bucket.
studies.author: TerraPulse Lab
